text.text:
Delicious, stuffed bagels that actually live up to the IG hype!
​I’ve been seeing Miopane all over my feed for months, but the massive lines kept putting me off. Today, I finally committed and arrived 15 minutes before opening—which landed me 4th in line. Word of warning: if you get there just 5 minutes later (10 mins before opening), you’ll easily be 16th or 17th. Arriving early is definitely the move.
​It isn't cheap—expect to pay around $50 for 7 bagels—but the quality and fillings make it worth it.
​The staff was incredibly welcoming. Since it was my first time, the employee walked me through their all-time favorites. I ended up trying a massive variety:
​Sweet: Matcha Crumble, Black Sesame
​Savory & Rich: Smoked Gouda, Garlic Butter, Onion Cream Cheese, Jalapeno Cheddar, Egg Breakfast
​Every single one of them was awesome. They are incredibly flavorful, perfectly textured, and generously stuffed. If you've been on the fence because of the social media hype, it's absolutely worth waking up early for!

text.text:
Miopane is amazing and definitely worth trying if you’re in Pasadena. Their savory bagels are the standout for me - soft, fluffy and really flavorful. I’ve tried the Garlic Butter, Everything Bagel with Smoked Gouda, and Jalapeño Sharp Cheddar, and all of them were great, but the Everything Bagel was my favorite. I tried the black sesame bagel, which was good, but I personally liked the savory ones more.

The maple glaze green onion croissant was also nice and worth trying if you enjoy the green onion flavor. It’s mix of sweet and savory without feeling too heavy.

Just be prepared for a line, especially on weekends. I went on a Saturday afternoon and waited about 30 minutes, but on a Friday afternoon it was only around 5 minutes. Plan accordingly, but overall it’s a great bakery and I’d definitely come back

text.text: Excellent assortment of baked goods including some interesting interpretations of bagels. Feels more like a bakery for a variety of pastries than it does a bagel shop. Creative and tasty interpretations of classic pastries, some Asian inspired items like the Green onion croissant which was so good. Loved the garlic onion bagel, matcha "bagel", elote bagel, pear Danish. Pro tip: ask to have your bagels/pastries warmed. They also keep well next day warmed in oven. The line trails outside the building simply to ensure the interior space isn't crowded so don't freak out if the line looks long. It moves fairly quickly.

text.text:
If you are a fan of bagels, Miopane is an absolute must-visit. I’ve tried many bakeries, but the texture and creativity here are on a completely different level.
The bagels have a chew on the outside but stay incredibly soft and fluffy on the inside.
​Incredible Fillings: They don’t hold back! The cream cheese fillings are generous and come in unique flavors. The matcha and blueberry cream cheese are my personal favorites. Perfectly balanced and not overly sweet.
The shop is clean, well-organized, and the staff is always efficient even when there’s a line. Which often there is a line for good reason.

text.text:
1. Black Sesame Bagel - unique, can’t find this combo anywhere
2. Everything Bagel with Smoked Gouda - liked me gouda
3. Sour Cream & Onion Bagel - liked the roasted garlic inside
4. Jalapeño Cheddar Bagel - most normal
5. Maple Glazed Green Onion Croissant

In order of how much I liked/thought unique. Lucked out with only 20 min wait. Would go back if I crave unique bagels. Their texture is fluffy and cream cheese already embedded. Maple glazed croissant is a bit too sweet for my taste. I forgot to try the matcha crumble.
